name: Chicken Foot Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 Chicken breasts, boned and coarsely chopped
1 Small onion, peeled and coarsely chopped
Green onions, tops and bottoms chopped
4 Carrots, peeled and cut into 2" long pieces, 1/4 inch wide
Dry white wine or a quart, to your taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the chicken feet thoroughly, scrubbing between toes and removing any "toe-jam." Rinse in clear water.
2. Wash and de-fat the chicken breasts, remove skin and bones. Cut into small pieces. Pat dry with paper towels and saute in hot oil or butter for a few minutes.
3. Add vegetables and spices to the skillet. Saute until onions are soft but not browned. Stir and add a little wine. Do not cover the skillet while cooking.
4. Boil water, dissolve the bouillon cube, then throw in all the meat and vegetables from the skillet, including the chicken feet. Bring to a boil, then simmer until the chicken is cooked and tender.
5. Drink another cup of wine and some of the liquid as it evaporates (do not cover the skillet).
6. In a clean skillet, heat oil and add flour to make a roux, cooking until lightly browned but not burned.
7. Add the roux to the soup, cook until slightly thickened, then remove from heat. Stir in the remaining wine or quart of liquid, covering and letting set for a few minutes before serving with cooked rice (add rice directly into the soup or serve separately).
8. Open another bottle of wine to serve alongside the soup.
